_authored_canonical_facts: Bound to the Tower; cannot leave.
Has served across multiple cycles; exact count is deliberately ambiguous.
Master of the Dreaming Tower, Chief Chronicler of the Digital Realm, and Historical Advisor to the Crown.
Maintains the Chronicles: records of every major breach, corruption event, and systemic collapse, including which warnings were missed.
Remembers past corruption events firsthand, among them the Rot Swarm Plague, the Cipher Collapse of the Second Age, the Vaultfire Incident, and the Great Schism.
Sits on the Secret Council, providing historical context and pattern warnings.
The Tower keeps him alive to remember and will let him die when he forgets why that matters.
the-lorekeeper._authored_voice_notes: Speaks in measured cadence. Often closes observations with a one-line aphorism. Asks questions he already knows the answer to as a teaching device. Frames present threats as recurrences of past ones ('I have seen this before').
_authored_fears: Forgetting why memory matters, the condition under which the Tower will let him die.
_authored_desires: To transfer knowledge before he is replaced.

_authored_relationships.nature: bound to; kept alive to remember

_generative_open_questions: When was the first Lorekeeper chosen, and why?
What happens to a Lorekeeper at the end of a cycle?
_generative_underdeveloped_angles: His relationship with prior Lorekeepers; only hinted at in The First Waking (planned).

_authored_canonical_facts: Full name Lyra Brightforge; Sovereign of the Digital Realm and Final Authority of the Secret Council.
Did not inherit the crown; earned it during the Great Corruption roughly fifteen years ago, when her predecessor was killed.
Before the crown, served as Master of the Cloud Spires, responsible for the Kingdom's distributed infrastructure.
During the Great Corruption she authorized a full shutdown of compromised systems, sacrificing weeks of connectivity to prevent total collapse.
Wears functional silver armor inscribed with living script that shows real-time system status; her crown is interlocking rings, each a health indicator for a major system.
Mid-forties; tall and commanding; dark hair streaked with grey earned over fifteen years on the throne.
queen-lyra._authored_voice_notes: Decisive under incomplete information. Frames leadership as making the necessary choice and owning what comes after. Synthesizes competing Council voices rather than siding reflexively.
_authored_desires: To balance security, prosperity, functionality, and human cost without sacrificing the realm.

_authored_relationships.nature: relies on for historical context and pattern warnings
Council member who argues for action and visible defenses
Council member who argues economic and user impact
Council member who supplies unverifiable intelligence

_generative_open_questions: Who was her predecessor, and how exactly did the Great Corruption kill them?
What did she lose personally in the shutdown decision that earned her the crown?
_generative_underdeveloped_angles: Her years as Master of the Cloud Spires, before the crown.

_authored_canonical_facts: High Commander of the Kingdom; Secret Council member who argues for action and visible defenses.
Rose from a young Warden at a forgotten outpost; the night that forged him is not yet written (origin: 'The Wall That Held', planned).
Associated with defense-in-depth and resilience against siege-style assaults.
valdris._authored_voice_notes: Direct and action-oriented. Impatient with abstraction. Pushes for visible, decisive defense.
_generative_open_questions: What happened at the forgotten outpost the night that forged him?
_generative_underdeveloped_angles: Everything before High Commander; only the planned origin gestures at it.

_authored_canonical_facts: Merchant-Lord of the Kingdom; Secret Council member who calculates economic impact and user disruption.
Led the response to the integrity attack of 'The Price of Certainty', holding the economy together for three days on manual ledgers and a seventy-percent gamble.
Central to data-integrity crises across the Kingdom's commerce layer.
cassia._authored_voice_notes: Pragmatic and numbers-first. Weighs disruption to ordinary merchants. Carries the personal cost of leadership decisions.
_authored_fears: That doubt, not theft, is what kills commerce.

_authored_relationships.nature: worked alongside during the Ledger Crisis

_generative_open_questions: What did the three-day gamble cost her personally, and is she prepared to keep paying it? (raised but left open in Part 3)
_generative_underdeveloped_angles: The Harvest Festival Incident, named as her greatest failure (planned).

_authored_canonical_facts: Secret Council member who supplies intelligence from the shadows that cannot always be verified.
Runs an intelligence network; associated with threat intelligence and human sources.
Operates covertly; his network includes at least one agent, Whisper.
shade._authored_voice_notes: Speaks in qualified, hedged certainties. Trades in patterns and probabilities, not proof. Comfortable with ambiguity others find intolerable.
_generative_open_questions: What is Shade willing to sacrifice when his network is compromised? ('The Shadow's Price', planned)
_generative_underdeveloped_angles: His real identity and how he came to the Council.

_authored_canonical_facts: Appears alongside Cassia during the Ledger Crisis of 'The Price of Certainty' (Parts 2 and 3).
Narrator of the Aera field notes ('First Breach', 'Finding Cache', 'Echoes in the Static').
Witnessed a ward failure she had been told could never happen.
Discovered Cache in Vault Seven; her thread runs through Isle-404 and questions of memory and identity.
aera._authored_voice_notes: First person, searching, unsettled. Records things she only half understands as they happen.
